rulings.Case Title: J.R. MARKETING, LLC., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Hartford Casualty Insurance Company To Dissolve Or Modify The "Enforcement Order" Entered By Sep-27-06
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 9, 2010, line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY Motion By Hartford Casualty Insurance Company To Dissolve Or Modify The "Enforcement Order" Entered By Sep-27-06. Motion denied. Long vs. Century Indemnity Co. (2008) 163 Cal.App. 4th 1460 is distinguishable in that the insurer provided a defense and did not breach its duty to defend. Where the insurer breaches its duty to defend, it forfeits the protections of Civil Code section 2860. Defendant's remedy is to pursue the reimbursement claim. No authority supports the posting of a bond. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: J.R. MARKETING, LLC., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY et al

rulings.Case Title: VIGILANT INSURANCE COMPANY VS. SELF-HELP FOR THE ELDERLY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 9, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT SELF-HELP FOR THE ELDERLY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion denied, failure to shift burden of proof. The Housekeeping agreement does not contain any language that extends insurance coverage to defendant. The terms of the insurance policy do not extend coverage to defendant. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREAS AMUNDIN VS. METHOD360, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPOPRATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 9, 2010, line 4, PLAINTIFF ANDREAS AMUNDIN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion against McKessor: denied. Triable issues of fact whether plaintiff was an employee or independent contractor, which is normally a question of fact. (See tentative ruling on McKesson's motion for Summary Judgment. Motion against Method360: denied. Triable issue of fact raised whether Blackstone case was based on plaintiff's conduct as a Method360 employee. Issue raised whether plaintiff was sued by Blackstone based on conduct occurring before Method360's involvement. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREAS AMUNDIN VS. METHOD360, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPOPRATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 9, 2010, line 4, DEFT MCKESSON CORP. MOTION FOR SUMM. JUDG. Motion for Summ. Judg. denied. Mtn. for Summ. Adj. denied as to the 1st cause of action for indemnification. Triable issues of fact raised whether Pltf. was an employee or independent contractor, which is normally a question of fact. Plft. was provided a badge for his workplace, a cubicle with a computer at McKessor's offices, received some direction from Afendakis & Roos, who told him how the project plan was to be executed, attended meetings & provided weekly status reports to McKesson.(Plft.'s Farradj's & Rao's depos.) Failure to shift burden of proof re: estoppel, no egregious facts represented. Blackstone case settled out of court, no showing that Plft."successfully" asserted independent contractor status. TIF whether McKessor benefited from Plft's work & is obligated to indemnify him for the damages he suffered in defending himself in the Blacksone case. (Plft's Decl.) Issue #4, declar. relief, denied, because of a triable issue of fact whether there is an indemnification claim. Issue #5 denied as redundent of 1st issue. Summ. Adj. denied as to 6th & 7th issues: Triable issue of fact whether the email exchange between Rao and Plft. created a contract.(Plft's Decl., Ex BB.) Mnt. denied as to 8th & 9th issues, wrongful termination, triable issue of fact whether Plft. was "fired" around the same time he asked McKessor to indemnify him on the Blackstone case (UMF 75, Plft's Decl, Louderback Decl. Ex.N) Mtn. grt'd as to 5th & 6th causes of action for fraud and negligent misrepresentation.(Issues 10 & 11). No evid. of an intent to induce reliance. No evid. presented that anyone at McKesson intended to induce Plft. to rely on specific representations concerning terms of his employment to his detriment. Mtn. grt'd as to 7th & 8th causes of action, infliction of emotional distress (Issues 12 & 13.)No evid.that deft's conduct transcended the bonds of behavior tolerated by societ

rulings.Case Title: BEL AIR TIME CORPORATION, A CORPORATION VS. PUFFINS, A PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 9, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT ALI SABAHI MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Motion denied without prejudice. Ali Sabahi is not an attorney and lacks standing to vacate the default against Ruffins and Debra Sabahi. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NORMAN JAMES BELISLE VS. DAVID A. SHERMAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 9, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANT DAVID SHERMAN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled regarding standing issue. Executrix's final account and report describes her actions in item 7 assigning the rights of the estate to plaintiff. Judge approved of executrix's actions in order dated Sepatemer 29, 2009. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th, 7th and 8th causes of action. Insufficient facts pled that defendant intentionally disrupted or breached a relationship between plaintiff and Wells Fargo, or that a relationship existed. Fraud must be supported by specific facts, and constructive fraud must be shown by misleading conduct or an inherently fraudulent act or omission. Facts pled do not show defendant's retention of the insurance proceeds was unjust. Plaintiff does not allege facts sufficient to show he had possession of the insurance proceeds or that the dispostion was wrongful. Plaintiff must allege wrongful acts defendant performed to obtain the loan and receive the insurance proceeds. =(302/CWW)
